title: androsaemum calycinum
intro: Androsaemum calycinum, also known as the spiny creeping thyme or Creeping Spindleberry, is a flowering plant native to Japan and the Korean Peninsula. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits of Androsaemum calycinum include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and possess antioxidant properties. It is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
